mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are significantly positive when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance in event studies.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint exceedance of 12-month moving averages yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the regime-conditional coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL reading above 330.0 indicates entrenched inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 330.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k and UNRATE declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction effect is subsumed by GDPC1 growth in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to stronger corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window, or GDPC1 growth shows no significant correlation with 20-day forward returns after adjusting for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain bottlenecks that disrupt earnings momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines > 1.0% during recovery phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.
